subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and responding to stories and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child will act out different verbs that mean similar actions to understand their slight differences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).
description_plain: Your child can find real-life examples of words and how they are used, like noticing colorful places at school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child will show they understand common verbs and adjectives by knowing their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort everyday objects into groups, like shapes or foods, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child will explore how words relate to each other and their different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: With support, your child can use common word endings and beginnings (like -ed, -s, re-, un-) to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).
description_plain: Your child can learn new meanings for words they already know and use them correctly, like knowing 'duck' is a bird and also a verb meaning to lower the head.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what new or tricky words mean by using what they read and learn in kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2.
description_plain: Begins in grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child can spell simple words by sounding them out and matching sounds to letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can write letters that match most consonant and short vowel sounds they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks at the end of sentences, like periods and question marks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child can use capital letters at the start of sentences and for the word 'I'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can say and make longer complete sentences when working together with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child can use common words that show location or direction, like 'in,' 'on,' and 'with.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child can understand and use question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).
description_plain: Your child can say the plural form of words by adding sounds like /s/ or /es/, for example, 'dogs' or 'wishes.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns (naming words) and action words (verbs).

subject: Language Arts
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print many uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses basic grammar rules when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.
description_plain: Your child speaks clearly so others can hear and understand their thoughts and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions as desired to provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to their descriptions to give more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events and, with prompting and support, provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can describe people, places, things, and events they know and add more details when helped.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to get help, learn new information, or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.
description_plain: Your child shows they understand stories or information by asking and answering questions and asking for help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by taking turns talking several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for talking with others, like listening and taking turns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child joins in conversations with different people about kindergarten topics and stories in small or large groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: Begins in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).
description_plain: With help, your child can join in simple research and writing projects, like reading books by a favorite author and sharing their thoughts.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, explore a variety of digital tools in participating in a production of a published writing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child can try using different digital tools to help create a finished piece of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support, orally respond to questions and suggestions from adults and peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child can answer questions and suggestions out loud and add more details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story about one or more events by drawing, speaking, and writing, putting the events in order and sharing how they feel about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, speaking, and writing to explain a topic by naming it and giving some information about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (e.g., My favorite book is . . .).
description_plain: Your child can share their opinion about a topic or book by drawing, speaking, and writing, telling what they like or think.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read simple books carefully and understand what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look alike by listening to the different sounds in the letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).
description_plain: Your child can read common words by sight, like 'the,' 'you,' and 'is.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child knows the long and short sounds for the five main vowels and their common spellings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary or many of the most frequent sounds for each consonant.
description_plain: Your child can say the main sounds that letters make, especially consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change or add sounds in simple one-syllable words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonant-vowel-consonant, or CVC) words.(This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the beginning, middle, and ending sounds in simple three-letter words like 'cat' or 'dog.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can put together and break apart parts of words that rhyme and sound alike.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count and say the parts (syllables) in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands how words are made up of sounds and parts like syllables.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child understands that words are separated by spaces when reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words match specific letters in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right and top to bottom.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and what it looks like.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can join group reading activities and understand what is being read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify similarities and differences between two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: With help, your child can find ways two books about the same topic are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the reason(s) an author gives to support point(s) in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can find reasons the author gives to support their ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, make connections between the illustrations and the text.
description_plain: With help, your child can connect pictures in a book to the words on the page.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, define the role of the author and the illustrator of a text and present the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain who the author and illustrator are and share the main ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can find the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a book are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main topic of a book and share important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group reading and understand the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell how characters in stories have similar or different adventures and experiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, make connections between the illustrations in the story and the text.
description_plain: With help, your child can connect the pictures in a story to the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, define the role of the author and the illustrator of a story in telling the story.
description_plain: With help, your child can explain what the author and illustrator do to tell the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts (e.g., storybooks, poems).
description_plain: Your child can recognize different kinds of texts, like storybooks and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is in the story, where it happens, and what big things happen, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: Your child can retell familiar stories and include important details, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a story, with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., because).
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including words like 'because' to show simple ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words and describing words by explaining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can notice how words relate to real life, like pointing out cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can explain what words mean by naming their group and important details, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups, like colors or clothes, to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child shows they understand how words relate to each other and have different meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common root words and their different forms, like 'look,' 'looks,' and 'looking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of frequently occurring affixes (prefixes and suffixes) to interpret the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common beginnings and endings of words to help figure out what a word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 2.
description_plain: Begins in grade 2.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell new words by sounding them out and using spelling rules they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child spells common words and tricky words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly in dates and when listing words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child uses periods, question marks, and exclamation points at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of people and dates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand simple and compound sentences that tell, ask, command, or show strong feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child uses common words that show location or time, like during, beyond, and toward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child uses words like a, an, the, this, and that to point out nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child uses common connecting words like and, but, or, so, and because.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child uses common describing words (adjectives).

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child uses verbs to talk about things that happened in the past, are happening now, or will happen in the future.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal and possessive pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their).
description_plain: Your child uses words like I, me, my, they, them, and their correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child uses singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child uses common nouns, names of people or places, and shows possession with nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Legibly print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can neatly print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task, audience, and situation.
description_plain: Your child speaks in complete sentences when it fits the situation and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child adds drawings or pictures to help explain ideas, thoughts, and feelings when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events with details, using clear words to share ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone says to learn more or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand better when something is confusing during talks about stories or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child will join conversations by listening and responding to what others say, having back-and-forth talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child understands and follows simple rules for talking with others, like listening carefully and taking turns to speak.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child will take part in group talks with classmates and adults about first-grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: Begins in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember or find information from experiences or sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
description_plain: Your child will work with others to research and write projects, like reading 'how-to' books and writing steps to do something.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With support, your child can use computers or other tools to write and share their work, learning to print, write cursive, or type.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With guidance, your child focuses on a topic, listens to ideas from friends, and adds details to make their writing better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories that tell about two or more events in order, include details, use words that show time order, and finish the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write to explain a topic by naming it, giving some facts, and ending the writing clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write their opinion by saying the topic or book, giving a reason for their opinion, and ending their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text out loud accurately, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads common tricky words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child reads words with endings that change their meaning, like -ed or -s.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child breaks two-syllable words into parts to read them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows that every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows that a silent e or vowel pairs can make a long vowel sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can read one-syllable words that follow common spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read common two-letter sounds like ch, sh, and th.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can say all the individual sounds in a one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in a one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say a one-syllable word by putting together sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands spoken words, syllables, and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child knows what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works and the basic parts of books and texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support read informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read informational texts that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find simple ways two texts about the same topic are alike or different, like pictures or descriptions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the reasons an author gives to support ideas in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a text to explain the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child knows the difference between information from pictures and information from words in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child uses parts of a book like headings, tables of contents, and glossaries to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a text and tell important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read prose and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child reads stories and poems that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can tell how characters’ adventures and experiences are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a story to describe characters, places, or events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the big differences between storybooks and books that give facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or help imagine senses like sight or sound.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, places, and important events in a story using key details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and understand the central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child understands the main message or lesson of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories and include important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using adjectives and adverbs to describe (e.g., When other kids are happy that makes me happy).
description_plain: Your child uses new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including describing words like adjectives and adverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among closely related verbs (e.g., toss, throw, hurl) and closely related adjectives (e.g., thin, slender, skinny, scrawny).
description_plain: Your child notices small differences in the meanings of similar verbs and adjectives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe foods that are spicy or juicy).
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to real-life things, like describing foods as spicy or juicy.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words are related and can notice small differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries and beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries and simple dictionaries, in print or online, to find out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of the meaning of individual words to predict the meaning of compound words (e.g., birdhouse, lighthouse, housefly; bookshelf, notebook, bookmark).
description_plain: Your child guesses the meaning of compound words by knowing the meanings of the smaller words inside them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., addition, additional).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known prefix is added to a known word (e.g., happy/unhappy, tell/retell)
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes to words they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 2 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child uses different ways to figure out the meaning of new or tricky words when reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare formal and informal uses of English.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between formal and informal ways of speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use simple dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling mistakes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Generalize learned spelling patterns when writing words (e.g., cage → badge; boy → boil).
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns they have learned to write new words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use an apostrophe to form contractions and frequently occurring possessives.
description_plain: Your child can use apostrophes to make contractions and show possession in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in greetings and closings of letters.
description_plain: Your child knows to use commas in the greetings and closings of letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize holidays, product names, and geographic names.
description_plain: Your child capitalizes the names of holidays, products, and places on maps.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce, expand, and rearrange complete simple and compound sentences (e.g., The boy watched the movie; The little boy watched the movie; The action movie was watched by the little boy).
description_plain: Your child can write and change simple and compound sentences to add more detail or change the order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child uses describing words (adjectives) and words that tell how something is done (adverbs) correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the past tense of frequently occurring irregular verbs (e.g., sat, hid, told).
description_plain: Your child knows how to use past tense forms of common irregular verbs like 'sat' or 'told'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use reflexive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves) and indefinite pronouns (e.g., anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'myself' and 'anyone' correctly in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use frequently occurring irregular plural nouns (e.g., feet, children, teeth, mice, fish).
description_plain: Your child uses irregular plural nouns like 'feet' and 'children' properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use collective nouns (e.g., group).
description_plain: Your child uses words that name groups of things, like 'group'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word rules when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task, audience, and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in complete sentences to explain or give details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create audio recordings of stories or poems with the guidance and support from adults and/or peers; add drawings or other visual displays to stories or recounts of experiences when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: With help, your child can record stories or poems and add pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Tell a story or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking audibly in coherent sentences.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story or share an experience using facts and details, speaking clearly in full sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to clarify comprehension, gather additional information, or deepen understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about what someone says to better understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount or describe key ideas or details from a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child can retell or describe important ideas or details from stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask for clarification and further explanation as needed about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask for more explanation when they don’t understand what is being talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by linking their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can join conversations by adding comments that connect to what others have said.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, like waiting their turn, listening carefully, and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 2 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group conversations about grade 2 topics with classmates and adults.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: Begins in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: Your child can remember information from experiences or find facts from sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., read a number of books on a single topic to produce a report; record science observations).
description_plain: Your child can work with others on research and writing projects, like reading books on one topic to make a report.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With help, your child can use digital tools to create and share writing, and practice printing, cursive, or typing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults and peers, focus on a topic and strengthen writing as needed by revising and editing.
description_plain: With support, your child can focus on a topic and improve their writing by revising and editing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 3.
description_plain: Begins in grade 3.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount a well-elaborated event or short sequence of events, include details to describe actions, thoughts, and feelings, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about events with details about actions and feelings, use words that show order, and end with a closing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they introduce a topic, use facts and definitions to develop points, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that introduce a topic, give facts and definitions, and end with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply reasons that support the opinion, use linking words (e.g., because, and, also) to connect opinion and reasons, and provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that state a topic and opinion, give reasons, use linking words, and finish with a conclusion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a tricky word to figure out or fix how to say or understand it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books out loud clearly, at a good speed, and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads books at their grade level with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words with inconsistent but common spelling-sound correspondences.
description_plain: Your child can spot words that have unusual but common ways of matching letters to sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common prefixes and suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common beginnings or endings added.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled two-syllable words with long vowels.
description_plain: Your child can read two-syllable words with long vowel sounds that follow regular spelling patterns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know spelling-sound correspondences for additional common vowel teams.
description_plain: Your child knows how to read vowel teams (two vowels that make one sound) in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long and short vowels when reading regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can hear and tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in simple words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to sound out and read words at their grade level.

subject: Language Arts
description: Mastered in grade 1.
description_plain: Mastered in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: Mastered in grade 1.
description_plain: Mastered in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 2–3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade 2–3 informational texts about history, science, and technology, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points presented by two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can compare the main points from two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how reasons or evidence support specific points the author makes in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how reasons or facts in a text support what the author is saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific images (e.g., a diagram showing how a machine works) contribute to and clarify a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures or diagrams help explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main purpose of a text, including what the author wants to answer, explain, or describe.
description_plain: Your child can identify the main purpose of a text, like what the author wants to explain or describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., captions, bold print, subheadings, glossaries, indexes, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text efficiently.
description_plain: Your child can use text features like captions, bold words, and indexes to find important information quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 2 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of words and phrases in texts about grade 2 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic of a multi-paragraph text as well as the focus of specific paragraphs within the text.
description_plain: Your child finds the main topic of a text and the focus of each paragraph.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories and poetry, in the grades 2–3 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories and poems for 2nd and 3rd grade levels, using help when needed, and connect what they read to their own experiences or other stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more versions of the same story (e.g., Cinderella stories) by different authors or from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more versions of the same story by different authors or from different cultures and notice how they are alike and different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from the illustrations and words in a print or digital text to demonstrate understanding of its characters, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and words in books or on screens to understand the characters, where the story happens, and what happens in the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acknowledge differences in the points of view of characters, including by speaking in a different voice for each character when reading dialogue aloud.
description_plain: Your child understands that characters have different points of view and can show this by using different voices when reading their dialogue out loud.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure of a story, including describing how the beginning introduces the story and the ending concludes the action.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story is put together, explaining how the beginning starts the story and the ending finishes it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how words and phrases supply rhythm and meaning in a poem or song; determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how words and phrases create rhythm and meaning in poems or songs and understand their meaning in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how characters in a story respond to major events and challenges.
description_plain: Your child can describe how characters react to important events and challenges in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables and folktales from diverse cultures, and determine their central message, lesson, or moral.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories, including fables and folktales from different cultures, and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer such questions as who, what, where, when, why, and how to demonstrate understanding of key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions like who, what, where, when, why, and how to show they understand important details in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night we went looking for them).
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words for everyday talk, school subjects, and specific topics, including words that show time and place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child understands small differences in meaning between similar words that show feelings or how sure someone is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life situations, like describing friendly or helpful people.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and non-literal meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child knows when words or phrases mean exactly what they say and when they mean something different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how words relate to each other and notices small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child uses glossaries or simple dictionaries, in books or online, to find out what important words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child uses the meaning of a root word they know to help understand new words with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of new words by adding prefixes or suffixes to words they already know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the rest of the sentence to help understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or tricky words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written Standard English.
description_plain: Your child notices and follows the differences between how people speak and how writing is done in Standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases to make their writing more interesting or clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries or other resources to check and fix spelling when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child uses spelling rules and patterns, like word families and syllables, to write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).
description_plain: Your child spells common words and adds endings like -ing or -ed correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use possessives.
description_plain: Form and use possessives.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in addresses.
description_plain: Use commas in addresses.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child will capitalize the right words in titles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use and choose the correct forms of describing words and words that tell how things happen, like 'bigger' or 'most quickly.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure subjects and verbs agree and uses pronouns correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child uses different verb tenses like past, present, and future correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use regular and irregular verbs properly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child uses nouns that name ideas or feelings, like 'childhood.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make nouns plural, including those that don’t follow usual rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task, audience, and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences when needed to explain or give details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child makes interesting audio recordings of stories or poems that are easy to understand and adds pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child tells about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what a speaker says, adding details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details when listening to stories or information in different formats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask questions to understand what others say, stay focused on the topic, and connect their ideas to what others have shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child will follow simple rules during discussions, like waiting their turn to speak, listening carefully, and talking one at a time about the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or studying the material and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can join different kinds of group talks, listen to others, build on their ideas, and clearly share their own thoughts about grade 3 topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Begins in grade 4.
description_plain: Begins in grade 4.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences or finds facts from books and websites, takes short notes, and organizes the information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce and publish grade-appropriate writing, using technology, either independently or in collaboration with others.
description_plain: With help, your child can create and share writing using technology, alone or with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With adult support, your child writes pieces that are organized and fit the purpose of the task.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a sense of closure.
description_plain: Provide a sense of closure.

description_plain: Your child uses words like first, next, and finally to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by introducing characters or a narrator and organizes events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events with clear details and a clear sequence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear ending to their piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like also, and, and but to connect ideas in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child adds facts and details to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and groups related information together, using pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a topic clearly and share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a clear ending to their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'because' and 'for example' to connect opinions and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons to support their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, states an opinion, and organizes reasons to support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces that share their point of view and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text out loud accurately and with expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-appropriate words that are spelled in unusual ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode multi-syllable words.
description_plain: Decode multi-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child reads words with common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read new words.

description: Mastered in grade 1.

description_plain: By the end of the year, your child reads and understands grade 2–3 level nonfiction texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts the main points and details in two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child explains how sentences and paragraphs in a text are connected, like showing cause and effect or order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in a text to understand where, when, why, and how important events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the student's point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author’s point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses text features like headings and search tools to quickly find information on a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of important words related to grade 3 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child describes how events, ideas, or steps in a text are connected using words about time and cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, tell important details, and explain how those details support the main idea.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a text by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2–3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for third grade on their own and connect what they read to their own experiences or other texts.

subject: Language Arts
