description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child can compare and tell how themes, settings, and plots are alike or different in stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child can explain how pictures in a story help show feelings, characters, or settings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the student's point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the narrator’s or characters’ points of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child can talk or write about parts of stories, plays, or poems using words like chapter, scene, or stanza, and explain how each part adds to the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from non-literal language.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when words are used in a literal or a special way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters’ traits, feelings, and reasons for acting, and explain how their actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories like fables and myths from different cultures and explain the lesson or message using important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about a story by looking back at the text for answers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal precise actions, emotions, or states of being (e.g., quizzed, whined, stammered) and that are basic to a particular topic (e.g., wildlife, conservation, and endangered when discussing animal preservation).
description_plain: Your child learns and uses new words that are important for school subjects and that show clear actions, feelings, or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of words by relating them to their opposites (antonyms) and to words with similar but not identical meanings (synonyms).
description_plain: Your child understands words by knowing their opposites and similar words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child understands common sayings and explains what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the meaning of simple similes and metaphors (e.g., as pretty as a picture) in context.
description_plain: Your child explains simple comparisons like similes and metaphors in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child looks up words in dictionaries or online to find how to say them and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., telegraph, photograph, autograph).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., definitions, examples, or restatements in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues in the text to understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 4 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown words or words with more than one meaning by using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion).
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English and when informal talk is okay.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose punctuation for effect.
description_plain: Your child chooses punctuation to make writing clearer or more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases to convey ideas precisely.
description_plain: Your child picks words and phrases that clearly express their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when they write, speak, read, or listen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child spells grade-level words correctly and uses references if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma before a coordinating conjunction in a compound sentence.
description_plain: Your child uses a comma before words like 'and' or 'but' in compound sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks to mark direct speech and quotations from a text.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing someone’s exact words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correct capitalization.
description_plain: Use correct capitalization.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Correctly use frequently confused words (e.g., to, too, two; there, their).
description_plain: Your child can choose the right words when using commonly confused words like to, too, and two.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences, recognizing and correcting inappropriate fragments and run-ons.
description_plain: Your child can write complete sentences and fix sentences that are incomplete or too long.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use prepositional phrases.
description_plain: Your child can create and use prepositional phrases to add details in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Order adjectives within sentences according to conventional patterns (e.g., a small red bag rather than a red small bag).
description_plain: Your child knows how to put adjectives in the right order, like saying 'small red bag' instead of 'red small bag.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use modal auxiliaries (e.g., can, may, must) to convey various conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use words like can, may, and must to show different possibilities or rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the progressive (e.g., I was walking; I am walking; I will be walking) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use verb forms that show ongoing actions, like 'I am walking' or 'I was walking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use relative pronouns (who, whose, whom, which, that) and relative adverbs (where, when, why).
description_plain: Your child can use words like who, which, where, and when to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child understands and uses correct grammar and language rules when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task, audience, and situation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal language, like during presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small groups.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or videos to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can clearly tell about a topic, story, or experience using facts and details, speaking at a pace others can understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons and proof a speaker gives to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate information they hear or see in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain their own ideas and understanding after listening to a discussion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to understand better and add helpful comments during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules during discussions and do their assigned roles.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text").
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions].").
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, settings, or events in stories using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from grade-appropriate literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find important information from books or articles to help explain their thoughts or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child can remember or find information from books or the internet, take notes, organize it, and list where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, produce and publish grade-appropriate writing using technology, either independently or in collaboration with others.
description_plain: With some help, your child can write and share their work using computers or other technology.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child can plan, revise, and edit their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organize their ideas to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child can write an ending that fits the story or experience they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child can use clear words and details that help readers picture what happened.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'then,' 'next,' or 'finally' to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child can use conversations and descriptions to show what happened and how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child can start a story by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tell what happens in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to the information they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and special vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child can connect ideas using words like 'for example,' 'also,' or 'because.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, and examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child can introduce a topic clearly and group related information into paragraphs, using headings or pictures if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child can write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using words like 'for example' or 'in addition.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons that are backed up with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize their ideas in paragraphs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child can read stories and poems out loud with correct words, speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level texts with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads smoothly and accurately enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about letters, syllables, and word parts to read long or new words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Mastered in grade 1.
description_plain: Mastered in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: Mastered in grade 1.
description_plain: Mastered in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 4–5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level informational texts about history, science, and technical topics, with help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts on the same topic to talk or write about it clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in charts, graphs, or pictures and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child can compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event and explain how they focus on different details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain events or ideas in history, science, or technical texts, including what happened and why, using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how key details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in the grades 4–5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand grade-level stories, dramas, and poems, making connections to their own experiences or other texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child can compare how similar themes and story patterns are shown in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect what they read in a story or play to pictures or performances of it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child can compare different points of view in stories, like first-person versus third-person narration.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between poems, plays, and stories, and talk about parts like rhythm in poems or dialogue in plays.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language such as metaphors and similes.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including comparisons like similes and metaphors.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, setting, or event in detail using specific parts from the story or play.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme of a story, play, or poem using details from the text and summarize it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal contrast, addition, and other logical relationships (e.g., however, although, nevertheless, similarly, moreover, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new words that show ideas like contrast and addition, such as however, although, and moreover.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonyms, antonyms, homographs) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and explain the meaning of common idioms, adages, and proverbs.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and explain common sayings and proverbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figurative language, including similes and metaphors, in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figurative language like similes and metaphors in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation and determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries and other resources, both books and online, to find how to say and understand words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek and Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., photograph, photosynthesis).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek and Latin word parts to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., cause/effect relationships and comparisons in text) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the text, like cause and effect, to understand the meaning of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 5 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the varieties of English (e.g., dialects, registers) used in stories, dramas, or poems.
description_plain: Your child can compare different ways English is spoken or written in stories and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Expand, combine, and reduce sentences for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can make sentences longer, shorter, or clearer to make writing more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell grade-appropriate words correctly, consulting references as needed.
description_plain: Your child can spell grade-level words correctly and use references when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to indicate titles of works.
description_plain: Your child can use underlining, quotation marks, or italics to show titles of books, stories, or other works.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to set off the words yes and no (e.g., Yes, thank you), to set off a tag question from the rest of the sentence (e.g., It's true, isn't it?), and to indicate direct address (e.g., Is that you, Steve?).
description_plain: Your child can use commas correctly with words like yes and no, in questions, and when talking directly to someone.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate an introductory element from the rest of the sentence.
description_plain: Your child can use a comma after introductory words or phrases in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation to separate items in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas to separate items in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use correlative conjunctions (e.g., either/or, neither/nor).
description_plain: Your child can use pairs of connecting words like either/or and neither/nor correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in verb tense.
description_plain: Your child can notice and fix mistakes when verb tenses change incorrectly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verb tense to convey various times, sequences, states, and conditions.
description_plain: Your child can use verb tenses to show when things happen and how actions relate to each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the perfect (e.g., I had walked; I have walked; I will have walked) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use perfect verb tenses like 'I have walked' or 'I had walked' to show completed actions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections in general and their function in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands how conjunctions, prepositions, and interjections work in sentences and can explain their use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of Standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and sentence rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, using formal English when appropriate to task, audience, and situation.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak depending on the situation, using formal language when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, sound) and visual displays in presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, sounds, or other media to presentations to help explain main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text or present an opinion, sequencing ideas logically and using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can report on a topic or share an opinion clearly, using facts and details in order, and speak at a pace that is easy to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize the points a speaker makes and explain how each claim is supported by reasons and evidence.
description_plain: Your child summarizes what a speaker says and explains how the speaker supports their ideas with reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Summarize a written text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can summarize information from a text read aloud or from different types of media, like pictures, numbers, or spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and draw conclusions in light of information and knowledge gained from the discussions.
description_plain: Your child understands the main ideas shared in a discussion and can make conclusions based on what was talked about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions by making comments that contribute to the discussion and elaborate on the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer specific questions during a discussion and add to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows the agreed rules during discussions and does the roles they are assigned.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying the topic and uses that knowledge to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 5 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of discussions with others, listens to their ideas, and shares their own clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different subjects and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point[s]").
description_plain: Your child explains how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 5 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or a drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., how characters interact]").
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts characters, settings, or events in stories or plays using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from grade-appropriate literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child can find important information in books or articles to help explain their ideas or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; summarize or paraphrase information in notes and finished work, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child remembers or finds important information from experiences, books, or the internet, summarizes it, and lists where it came from.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that use several sources to build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects using several sources to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, produce and publish grade-appropriate writing using technology, either independently or in collaboration with others.
description_plain: Your child can write and share their work using computers or tablets, sometimes with help from adults or friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a different approach.
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, revises, edits, or rewrites their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows naturally from the story or events they describe.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and details that appeal to the senses to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words, phrases, and clauses to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'then,' 'next,' and 'after' to show the order of events in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, description, and pacing, to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child adds dialogue, descriptions, and controls the speed of the story to show what happens and how characters feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situation and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined experiences using good techniques, details, and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words and special vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within and across categories of information using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., in contrast, especially).
description_plain: Your child connects ideas using words like 'however' or 'especially' to show how information relates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, examples, or quotes to explain their topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, provide a general observation and focus, and group related information logically; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, organizes information in paragraphs, and uses headings or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing sentence or paragraph that sums up their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words, phrases, and clauses (e.g., consequently, specifically).
description_plain: Your child connects their opinion and reasons using words like 'because' or 'therefore.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide logically ordered reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child gives reasons in a clear order and supports them with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which ideas are logically grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic, states their opinion, and organizes ideas in paragraphs to explain their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces on topics or texts, giving reasons and information to support their view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level books with understanding and purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child uses knowledge of sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new, longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses grade-level phonics and word skills to figure out words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Mastered in grade 1.
description_plain: Mastered in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: Mastered in grade 1.
description_plain: Mastered in grade 1.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 4–5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child can read and understand challenging informational texts about history, science, and technical subjects on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from several texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from different books or articles about the same topic and use it to talk or write clearly about that topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text, identifying which reasons and evidence support which point(s).
description_plain: Your child understands how an author uses reasons and facts to support their ideas and can point out which reasons go with which ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Utilize information from multiple print or digital sources, demonstrating the ability to locate an answer to a question quickly or to solve a problem efficiently.
description_plain: Your child finds answers or solves problems quickly by using information from books or websites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze multiple accounts of the same event or topic, noting important similarities and differences in the point of view they represent.
description_plain: Your child can look at different stories about the same event and notice important similarities and differences in how people see it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in two texts.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts are organized, like by order of events, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 5 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of important words related to school subjects or topics they are learning about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the relationships or interactions between two or more individuals, events, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two or more people, events, or ideas are connected based on details in a history, science, math, or technical text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine two or more main ideas of a text and explain how they are supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find two or more main ideas in a text, explain how details support them, and tell the main points in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a text accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 4–5 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for their grade level on their own and connect what they read to their own experiences or other texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast stories in the same genre (e.g., mysteries and adventure stories) on their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories that are the same type, like mysteries or adventures, and talk about how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how visual and multimedia elements contribute to the meaning, tone, or aesthetics of a text (e.g., graphic novel, multimedia presentation of fiction, folktale, myth, poem).
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures, videos, or other media help tell a story or set the mood.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a narrator's or speaker's point of view influences how events are described.
description_plain: Your child understands how the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view changes the way events are described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how a series of chapters, scenes, or stanzas fits together to provide the overall structure of a particular story, drama, or poem.
description_plain: Your child can explain how chapters, scenes, or parts of a poem fit together to make the whole story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative language and connotative meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when they are used in a special way like similes, metaphors, or idioms.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast two or more characters, settings, or events in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., how characters interact).
description_plain: Your child can compare two or more characters, places, or events in a story by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text, including how characters in a story or drama respond to challenges or how the speaker in a poem reflects upon a topic; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem by looking at details and can tell the story in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Quote accurately from a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can quote parts of a story or poem accurately when explaining what it says and when making guesses based on the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words correctly and can research words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses the relationship between words, like cause and effect or part and whole, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification) in context.
description_plain: Your child understands and explains figures of speech, like when something is described as if it were a person.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word by looking at how it’s used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word or what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out what words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the sentence or paragraph around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies while reading or learning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child keeps the same style and tone when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child changes sentence types to make writing more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child knows and uses the rules of language when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell correctly.
description_plain: Spell correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child uses commas, parentheses, or dashes to add extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child notices when language is not standard and learns ways to improve their writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child finds and fixes unclear pronouns that don’t clearly show who or what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child notices and corrects when pronouns change in number or person inappropriately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'myself' or 'ourselves' to emphasize who is doing something.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).
description_plain: Your child uses pronouns correctly as subjects, objects, or to show possession.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct English grammar and language rules when writing or speaking and builds on what they learned before.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts, audience, and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on who they are talking to and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, music, or other media to presentations to help explain their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child shares ideas clearly and in order, using facts and details, while speaking with good eye contact and volume.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child identifies a speaker’s main argument and knows which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child understands information presented in different ways, like pictures or numbers, and explains how it relates to the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain different viewpoints by summarizing what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions to add to a discussion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, sets goals and deadlines, and knows their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying, and uses facts from that preparation to ask questions and think about ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group discussions, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and audiences, including time for research and revision.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not").
description_plain: Your child reads nonfiction and can find and think about the reasons and evidence that support the author’s ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres [e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories] in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics").
description_plain: Your child compares different types of stories or poems to see how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw relevant evidence from grade-appropriate literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds important information in books or articles to help explain their ideas or research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child can find information from books and websites, decide if the sources are trustworthy, and use the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can do short research projects by using several sources and changing their questions if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and publish grade-appropriate writing using technology, either independently or in collaborating with others.
description_plain: Your child writes and shares their work using computers or tablets, either alone or with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a different approach.
description_plain: With some help from others, your child can plan, revise, edit, and improve their writing or try a new way of writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and in an organized way that fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows naturally from the story or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, clear details, and descriptions that appeal to the senses to tell about experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child can use different words and phrases to show the order of events and changes in time or place in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child will use dialogue, pacing, and descriptions to make stories and characters more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
description_plain: Your child starts a story by setting the scene, introducing characters or narrator, and organizing events in order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear event order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that sums up the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses transition words to show how ideas and concepts connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will introduce a topic and organize information using methods like comparing or explaining causes, and use headings, charts, or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style when making an argument.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child can use words and phrases to clearly show how their reasons support their main point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources and shows they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claims and organizes reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child can write arguments that support their claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child will read and understand nonfiction books and articles for grades 6 to 8, with help if needed for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).
description_plain: Your child can compare how two different authors tell the same story or event.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the reasons and evidence an author uses to support their ideas, and tell which ideas are not supported.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child can use information from pictures, charts, and words together to understand a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what the author’s opinion or goal is and explain how the author shows it in the writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special or creative meanings, and explain how word choice changes the meaning or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an important person, event, or idea is introduced and described with examples in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important parts of a text to explain what it says and what it means beyond the words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6–8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems for grades 6 to 8, with some help for harder parts, and make connections to their own life, other texts, and the world.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare stories or poems that are different types but talk about similar ideas or themes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Not applicable to literature.
description_plain: Not applicable to literature.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author shows the narrator’s or speaker’s point of view in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole story or poem and helps develop the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in stories or poems, including special meanings and sounds, and explain how word choice affects the mood or feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how the plot of a story or play happens in parts and how the characters change or react as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme or main idea of a story or poem, explain how details show it, and summarize the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite relevant textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use important parts of stories or poems to explain what they say and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use grade-appropriate academic and subject-specific words and can research new words on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child understands the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how 'polite' and 'condescending' mean different things even if they seem related.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, and mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of figures of speech, like sayings or references, by looking at how they are used in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at the sentence or using a dictionary to be sure.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in books or online, to find how to say a word or what it exactly means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out what new words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses the words around a new word and its place in a sentence to guess what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
